channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How to develop a more secure attachment style for better health, happiness and relationships
item.description: Psychiatrist Amir Levine, co-author of the bestselling book Attached, has published a follow-up titled Secure. Drawing on 16 years of reader feedback, neuroscience research and clinical practice, Levi...

item.title: Fire outbreak destroys over 2,000 bags of onions in Sokoto, leaving farmers in heavy loss
item.description: My people, e be like say another sad tori don land from Sokoto State. Many onion farmers wey work tire for months, planting and waiting for harvest, just wake up one day see their whole effort turn to...

item.title: Phil Gould criticises St George Illawarra Dragons' recent coaching appointments as short-term solutions
item.description: Phil Gould has taken aim at the St George Illawarra Dragons’ recent coaching decisions, arguing that the club has relied too heavily on short-term fixes rather than committing to a long-term strategy....

item.title: DOE Implements Uniform Pump Price Adjustments Amid National Energy Emergency
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has started regulating uniform adjustments in the country's pump prices to ensure fairness for consumers facing high fuel costs caused by the ongoing conflict in the Mid...

item.title: Flood recovery, gang arrest, and fishing permit restoration mark key developments in the Western Cape
item.description: Mop-up operations are under way across several informal settlements in the Western Cape after heavy weekend rains triggered flooding. Two cold fronts brought widespread downpours that left communities...

item.title: Letters to the Editor, April 20: Readers discuss leadership, politics, nature, transit, vaccines and AI
item.description: This letters page brings together several reader responses to recent Globe and Mail coverage, spanning politics, the environment, public health, work culture and technology. One letter argues that flo...

item.title: Tanzania Expands Rural Electrification Efforts to Reach All Hamlets by 2030
item.description: Tanzania is making steady progress in expanding electricity access to rural areas, with a clear national goal of connecting all hamlets to power by 2030. According to the Rural Energy Agency (REA), mo...

item.title: Sarah Strong's Parents: Allison Feaster and Danny Strong, Former Basketball Stars Who Shaped Her Career
item.description: Sarah Strong, one of the top young talents in American collegiate basketball playing for UConn, comes from a strong basketball family. Her parents, Allison Feaster and Danny Strong, are both former pr...

item.title: Darcy Moore nears Anzac Day return for Collingwood as Essendon lose Jordan Ridley to calf injury
item.description: In the latest AFL briefing, Western Bulldogs midfielder Tom Liberatore is progressing through standard concussion protocols following his latest head knock and is not required to appear before an inde...

item.title: Nagaland Board expected to announce Class 10 and 12 results around April 24
item.description: As per available information from the headline, the Nagaland Board of School Education (NBSE) is expected to declare the results for Class 10 and Class 12 examinations around April 24. However, the fu...

item.title: 700 years of rainfall history reconstructed from baobab tree rings in south-western Madagascar
item.description: Na team of scientists from palaeoecology and climate fields don carry out first ever tree ring rainfall record for Madagascar using ancient baobab trees. Dem bore core samples from four baobabs for di...

item.title: Trabzonspor settle for 1-1 draw against Başakşehir as Onuachu misses key chances in Turkish Super Lig title chase
item.description: Paul Onuachu and his Nigerian teammates featured prominently for Trabzonspor in their home match against İstanbul Başakşehir at Papara Park, but the team could not secure the much-needed victory in th...

item.title: Two teenagers arrested in connection with arson attack on Kenton United Synagogue in Harrow
item.description: A 17-year-old boy and a 19-year-old man have been arrested by the Metropolitan Police in connection with an arson attack on Kenton United Synagogue in Harrow, north-west London. The incident occurred ...

item.title: Former AfDB President Adesina Calls for Investment in Africa's Growing Economy After Receiving Lifetime Award
item.description: Dr. Akinwumi A. Adesina, the immediate past President of the African Development Bank Group, has received the prestigious African Lifetime Achievement Award in Accra, Ghana on 11th April 2026. In his ...

item.title: ASX trims losses as oil volatility and Middle East tensions weigh on markets
item.description: The Australian sharemarket showed resilience on Monday, trimming earlier losses despite ongoing volatility in global oil prices and heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. The S&P/ASX 200...

item.title: The rise of the female gaze in contemporary film, television and romantasy literature
item.description: The article explores the growing prominence of the female gaze in popular culture, which emphasises the agency, desires, and complex inner lives of women rather than portraying them as objects for the...

item.title: Tanzania Expands Rural Electrification and Clean Energy Efforts Towards 2030 Goal
item.description: Tanzania is making steady progress in expanding electricity access to rural areas as part of its plan to achieve universal electrification by 2030. The government, through the Rural Energy Agency (REA...

item.title: Government cuts fuel VAT to ease cost of living amid global oil price surge
item.description: Serikali imechukua hatua ya haraka kusaidia wananchi kukabiliana na gharama ya maisha inayopanda, hasa kutokana na ongezeko la bei ya mafuta duniani. Rais William Ruto alitangaza kupunguzwa kwa Value ...

item.title: Strait of Hormuz remains closed as Iran rejects talks and tensions disrupt global energy supply
item.description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains highly uncertain after Iran reversed its brief decision to reopen the critical waterway and rejected renewed peace talks with the United States. The strai...

item.title: Enforcement Directorate raids Kolkata Police DCP's residence and arrests real estate businessman in money laundering case linked to illegal land deals
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted raids on Sunday at the residence of Shantanu Sinha Biswas, a senior Deputy Commissioner of Police (Special Branch) in Kolkata Police, as part of a money laun...
